Title: Metabolic engineering of the serine/glycine network as a means to improve the nitrogen content of crops

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting/key control step of PPSB in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 3-phosphoglycerate to 3-phosphohydroxypyruvate in the first step of phosphorylated serine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool; initiates pathway leading to serine and downstream amino acids/protein accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Serine; protein; essential amino acids; starch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improved crop nutritional value through increased nitrogen/protein content
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis shoots; maize leaves, stems, kernels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In Arabidopsis, expressed in roots and up-regulated in shoots under elevated CO2; also induced by darkness according to cited work
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in Arabidopsis shoots and maize shoots; phenotypes assessed in Arabidopsis shoots and maize leaves, stems, kernels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes serine accumulation and increases protein content; in Arabidopsis also increases starch and attenuates elevated-CO2-induced decline of essential amino acids; in maize increases nitrogen/protein content and nitrogen/carbon ratio without increasing starch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; triple overexpression comparison; qRT-PCR; western blot; GC-MS metabolite profiling; protein/starch assays; elemental analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AtPGDH1 alone increased serine in Arabidopsis under ambient and elevated CO2. 2. AtPGDH1 overexpression increased multiple amino acids and protein content in Arabidopsis and mitigated elevated-CO2-associated decreases in essential amino acids. 3. In Arabidopsis, AtPGDH1 overexpression also increased starch, supporting coupling to carbon metabolism. 4. In maize, heterologous AtPGDH1 overexpression increased protein and nitrogen/carbon ratio in leaves and stems, especially under elevated CO2, without increasing starch. 5. Comparison with triple overexpression supports PGDH as the limiting step of PPSB in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtPGDH1 overexpression had stronger effect on starch than triple overexpression in Arabidopsis; one maize line with highest expression showed reduced growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for metabolic engineering to raise plant nitrogen/protein content, particularly under future elevated CO2; may be stacked with other PPSB genes for stronger amino acid effects under elevated CO2 or with sink/transport traits for grain improvement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible growth penalty in high-expressing maize line; starch increase in Arabidopsis may alter carbon partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Oryza sativa Ubiquitin 3 promoter in maize; NA in Arabidopsis lines in provided text

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; data suggest not limiting under ambient CO2 in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an amino group from glutamate to 3-phosphohydroxypyruvate to produce 3-phosphoserine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improved nutritional amino acid and protein content
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed through overexpression in Arabidopsis triple overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In combination with AtPGDH1 and AtPSP1 contributes to maintenance/elevation of serine and several essential amino acids under elevated CO2, but shows little additional effect over AtPGDH1 alone under ambient CO2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: triple overexpression with AtPGDH1 and AtPSP1; metabolite profiling; protein/starch assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Included in Arabidopsis triple PPSB overexpression lines. 2. Did not provide clear additional increase over AtPGDH1 alone under ambient CO2. 3. Under elevated CO2, triple overexpression lines showed similar or somewhat higher serine and better maintenance of several essential amino acids than AtPGDH1-only lines.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core gene because directly manipulated in triple overexpression lines, though not supported as the main limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking partner with PGDH1 under elevated CO2 or in sink tissues where full PPSB activity may be limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear added benefit under ambient CO2 in shoots

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; not limiting under ambient CO2 in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improved nutritional amino acid/protein content; serine required for pollen viability
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis shoots; maize pollen/male gametophyte context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ambient CO2; elevated CO2 (2000 ppm); serine supplementation in pollen germination medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Manipulated in Arabidopsis triple overexpression lines; in maize, endogenous PSP1 required in male gametophyte
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Arabidopsis triple overexpression contributes to serine/eAA maintenance under elevated CO2; maize PSP1 loss of function causes male gametophyte lethality and reduced pollen germination that is partially rescued by exogenous serine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis triple overexpression; maize Cas9 mutagenesis of ZmPSP1 ortholog; segregation analysis; reciprocal crosses; pollen germination assays; serine feeding
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In Arabidopsis, inclusion of PSP1 in triple overexpression did not add strong effect over PGDH1 alone under ambient CO2. 2. Under elevated CO2, triple overexpression including PSP1 better maintained serine and several essential amino acids. 3. In maize, mutation of PSP1 active-site region prevented recovery of homozygous mutants and caused zero/near-zero male transmission. 4. Heterozygous mutant pollen showed ~50% germination, improved by exogenous serine, indicating PPSB-derived serine is required for viable pollen development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Maize PSP1 gene identified as GRMZM2g165383; active SGG motif is essential; homozygous disruptive mutations appear lethal at gametophyte level
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important cautionary target: editing/disrupting PSP1 can compromise male fertility; controlled overexpression with other PPSB genes may help nutrient engineering, especially in tissues with low endogenous PPSB activity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes male sterility/gametophyte lethality in maize

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for pollen viability and male gametophyte function
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: serine supplementation in pollen germination medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Required in maize male gametophyte based on transmission phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function prevents viable male gametophyte transmission and impairs pollen germination, indicating maize PPSB is essential for pollen viability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cas9 mutagenesis; sequencing; segregation analysis; reciprocal crossing; pollen germination assay; serine rescue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPSP1 is the single identified maize PSP gene. 2. Active-site mutations in the SGG motif could not be recovered in homozygous state. 3. Heterozygous plants transmitted the mutant allele through the female but not the male gametophyte. 4. Exogenous serine improved pollen germination of heterozygotes, linking PSP1 function to serine supply in pollen.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutated motif encoded DVFLVSGGFRQMIK region; active-site SGG essential
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Do not disrupt ZmPSP1 in breeding for nutritional improvement unless male-sterility is desired; tissue-specific enhancement rather than knockout is advisable
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Male sterility/gametophyte lethality upon disruptive mutation
